subject: Mathematics
title: What Is a Sequence?
description: A quick, practical guide to What Is a Sequence?.

hook.text: 2, 4, 6, 8—what comes next? Your brain sees the pattern instantly. That's a sequence.

buildup.text: A sequence is an ordered list of numbers that follows a rule. The rule determines every term.

discovery.text: Some sequences are simple: add 2 each time. Others are bizarre: 1, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 13.

twist.text: Not all sequences have obvious patterns. Some require deep math to describe their rule.

climax.text: Sequences model growth, decay, rhythm, and chaos. They appear in every branch of mathematics.

punchline.text: Numbers in a row, following a rule. That's all a sequence is.

quiz.question: What defines a mathematical sequence?
options: An ordered list of numbers following a rule
A set of numbers with no repetition
